patches_reviewed.subject: Re: [LSF/MM/BPF TOPIC][RFC PATCH v4 00/27] Private Memory Nodes (w/ Compressed RAM)
Re: [PATCH V10 00/10] famfs: port into fuse
patches_reviewed.patch_summary: The patch addresses performance issues in iomap_begin() by avoiding unnecessary lookup operations and instead using cached metadata to access DAX devices. It also introduces a new BPF operation, dax_fmap_parse(), which allows filesystems to perform filesystem-specific setup and populate the DAX bitmap. The patch requires no hard-coded OMF structures in the FUSE API and ensures that metadata is accessible with a single pointer dereference.

review_comments.author: Joanne Koong
review_comments.summary:
Reviewer Joanne Koong noted that Gregory's patch does not handle GET_DAXDEV, which is necessary to validate the addressing information for DAX devices, and suggested pairing it with John's GET_FMAP/GET_DAXDEV operations.

Reviewer Joanne Koong questioned the necessity of lazy initialization of DAX devices via GET_DAXDEV on file open, suggesting it would be more efficient to validate and acquire devices during iomap configuration at initialization time, potentially simplifying kernel-side logic and improving FUSE_OPEN performance.

Reviewer Joanne Koong suggested integrating the bpf approach with Darrick's fuse-iomap work, renaming dax_fmap_parse() to iomap_setup() to make it a generic interface for userspace setup, and noted that the distinction between dax and non-dax should be handled by the fuse iomap plumbing.

Reviewer Joanne Koong suggested repurposing the bpf_dax_fmap_parse() function into a generic kfunc, bpf_fuse_iomap_get_inode_meta(), that returns a bounded pointer to cached inode metadata for both dax and non-dax cases.

Reviewer Joanne Koong suggested removing the `file_size` field from the `fuse_dax_fmap_ops` structure, as it can be derived from `i_size_read(inode)`

Reviewer suggested simplifying GET_DAXDEV and making GET_FMAP generic by introducing FUSE_IOMAP_GETMAP, allowing servers to control fetching of mapping blobs on open.

Reviewer Joanne Koong expressed a preference for a different solution to the problem of accessing metadata in iomap_begin(), suggesting that adding generic stride/offset multi-device support to fs/iomap would be the cleanest approach and could potentially solve multiple issues at once.
review_comments.raw_body:
Thanks John for running the benchmarks on your hardware. And thanks
Gregory for your work on this too.

---

It handles #3 by removing GET_DAXDEV as a fuse op and having the
daxdev initialization / setup routed through FUSE_IOMAP_CONFIG at
iomap initialization time instead, which integrates with the generic
iomap infrastruture/uapi additions Darrick added in his fuse-iomap
series [1].

In this series the GET_DAXDEV op gets sent lazily on file opens but
it's still not clear to me why this is necessary. imo device setup
should happen logically as part of iomap configuration and it seems
more efficient to have devices validated/acquired before any files are
opened. I thihnk that makes things a lot simpler on the kernel side in
other ways (eg we can get rid of famfs_dax_devlist / famfs_daxdev /
famfs_devlist_sem / famfs_update_daxdev_table() /
famfs_fuse_get_daxdev() altogether). It also saves the famfs server
the roundtrip context switching cost if we get rid of GET_DAXDEV and
move it to iomap initialization time, which will improve FUSE_OPEN
performance for famfs. Maybe there's something I'm missing here as to
why the daxdev initialization has to be done lazily on open?

I think Darrick had also mentioned something earlier about how he
thinks GET_DAXDEV should be another application of backing files [2] -
I like this idea too, as it gets rid of the GET_DAXDEV op and reuses
fuse's existing infrastructure.

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/linux-fsdevel/177188734695.3935739.8198854011004837207.stgit@frogsfrogsfrogs/
[2] https://lore.kernel.org/linux-fsdevel/20260416224331.GD114184@frogsfrogsfrogs/

---

Just a note for later, if the bpf approach gets pursued further:
instead of making this a dax specific ops, I think this needs to be
integrated interface-wise with Darrick's fuse-iomap work since he does
the same thing. I think dax_fmap_parse() could be renamed to something
like iomap_setup(), where userspace can use this to do any sort of
generic setup, whether that's mapping related or dax related or not.
In my mind, the dax vs non dax distinction is handled by the fuse
iomap plumbing that chooses which iomap entry points to call,  but
beyond that,  the callbacks and struct ops themselves should be
generic enough to be shared between the two.

---

another note for later, if the benchmarks prove promising and after
the LSF discussions we decide to go with this approach: imo we
could/should repurpose this into a generic
bpf_fuse_iomap_get_inode_meta() that returns a bounded pointer into
whatever opaque blob was cached on the inode during iomap_setup(),
where it'd be a generic kfunc serving both the dax and non-dax case
for any kind of mapping layout

---

I don't think file_size is needed here? seems like we could just
derive this from i_size_read(inode)?

---

I think we could kill GET_DAXDEV and for GET_FMAP, we could make this
a generic FUSE_IOMAP_GETMAP where the server can set a flag on open to
indicate whether the mapping blob should be fetched or not.

---

I don't have a NACK on what you wrote above, thank you for your work
on this and bridging it into John's famfs server.

As I understand it, Amir also scheduled a cross-track FS+MM+IO session
at LSF to discuss famfs and dax iomap. Christoph had posted a
suggestion in another message about solving this problem with adding
generic stride/offset multi-device support to fs/iomap, and I'm hoping
the LSF session will shed more light on this, as that to me seems the
cleanest solution and would pretty much give everyone what they want
(including getting famfs unblocked, as I think with this approach we
would just need to figure out the generic stride/offset format for the
fuse iomap uapi, and could have the interleaving logic living in fuse
initially with fs/iomap migrations done post-merge). In the meantime,
I think it's really helpful getting the data points on how bpf
performs, thank you for running the benchmarks on your setup, John.

Thanks,
Joanne

patches_acked.subject: Re: [PATCH] NFSD: Increase the default max_block_size to 4MB

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v1 17/17] docs: fuse: document extended passthrough (FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO)

version_history.subject: [PATCH v1 17/17] docs: fuse: document extended passthrough (FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO)

series_items.subject: [PATCH v1 00/17] fuse: extend passthrough to inode operations
series_items.patch_summary: This patch series extends FUSE passthrough to support inode operations, including getattr, setattr, and readdir. It introduces a new mode, FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO, which requires one-to-one inode number mapping and allows attaching a backing file for the lifetime of the fuse inode. The patches also prepare for passthrough of additional operations, such as rename and unlink.

review_comments.author: Joanne Koong
review_comments.summary: Identified a double-put bug in fuse_try_move_page() where the folio refcount drops to zero prematurely. Suggested adding an extra folio_get() before replace_page_cache_folio() to maintain the reference.

review_comments.author: Joanne Koong
review_comments.summary: Added an arg for a struct fuse_statx pointer to fuse_iget() and passed it through to fuse_change_attributes_i() so the statx attributes get persisted in cache.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v1 01/17] fuse: introduce FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO mode
series_items.patch_summary: This patch introduces FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO mode, a stricter variant of the existing FUSE_PASSTHROUGH mode. In this new mode, the kernel ensures that fuse inodes are uniquely identified by their backing file inode number, preventing multiple fuse inodes from being mapped to the same backing file unless they are hardlinks. This is only supported on 64-bit architectures where the inode number can be a u64 value. The patch modifies several files in the fs/fuse directory to implement this new mode.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v1 02/17] fuse: prepare for passthrough of inode operations
series_items.patch_summary: This patch prepares the fuse file system to pass through inode operations by introducing a new field, ops_mask, in the FUSE_DEV_IOC_BACKING_OPEN ioctl input argument. This allows for the attachment of a backing file to a fuse inode without an open file, enabling passthrough of some inode operations. The patch also adds checks for non-regular backing files and requires a filesystem with FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO support for setting up passthrough of any inode operations.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v1 03/17] fuse: prepare for readdir passthrough on directories
series_items.patch_summary: This patch prepares the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) kernel module to support readdir passthrough on directories by allowing the inode iomode state to be applicable to directory inodes and modifying the fuse_sync_release() helper function to handle directories. The goal is to improve performance by reducing the need for parallel dio operations, which are typically required for regular files. The patch makes several changes to various FUSE-related files, including cuse.c, dir.c, file.c, fuse_i.h, inode.c, and iomode.c.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v1 04/17] fuse: implement passthrough for readdir
series_items.patch_summary: This patch implements passthrough for readdir operations in the FUSE file system. It allows a directory to be opened in passthrough mode, where the client requests the server to perform the readdir operation instead of the kernel. This is done by setting the FOPEN_PASSTHROUGH flag when opening the directory and requesting the READDIR inode operation. The patch also ensures that a directory cannot be opened for both cached readdir and passthrough at the same time. The opt-in nature of passthrough means that there will be no READDIRPLUS call to the server, which can result in performance overhead for certain use cases like ls -l.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v1 05/17] fuse: prepare for long lived reference on backing file
series_items.patch_summary: This patch prepares the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) kernel module to support long-lived references to backing files. Currently, a backing file is attached and detached on each open and close operation. To enable attaching a backing file without an open file, this patch introduces a new flag for long-lived backing file attachment and modifies several functions to handle this new behavior.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v1 06/17] fuse: implement passthrough for getattr/statx
series_items.patch_summary: This patch implements passthrough functionality for the getattr and statx operations in the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) kernel module. The patch allows the FUSE inode attributes to be updated directly from the backing inode's attributes, rather than relying on cached values. This is achieved by calling vfs_getattr() on the backing inode to respond to user requests for statx(2). The patch also introduces a new function fuse_kstat_to_attr() that converts kstat data into fuse_attr structures. The passthrough functionality is enabled by default and can be disabled using the no_statx mount option.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v1 07/17] fuse: prepare to setup backing inode passthrough on lookup
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds a helper function to associate a backing inode for inode passthrough operations in the FUSE file system. The helper, fuse_backing_id_get, retrieves a fuse backing object by its ID and increments its reference count to store it in the fuse inode. This is part of setting up backing inode passthrough on lookup response. The minimal requirement for the backing inode is to support GETATTR passthrough operations. Unlike open operations, this mapping is created for the entire lifetime of the inode and cannot be changed later.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v1 08/17] fuse: add passthrough ops gating
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds passthrough operation gating to the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) kernel module. It introduces a check to verify whether passthrough operations can be called for a specific file or not, based on the FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO mode being enabled. This preserves backwards compatibility with prior passthrough behavior and ensures that the correct path is taken when accessing files. The patch modifies several existing callers in the FUSE codebase to route through this new check, allowing for more controlled and secure handling of passthrough operations.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v1 09/17] fuse: prepare to cache statx attributes from entry replies
series_items.patch_summary: This patch prepares the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) kernel module to cache statx attributes from entry replies by adding a new argument to the fuse_iget function and passing it through to fuse_change_attributes_i. This change is a prerequisite for a future patch that will add a new struct fuse_entry2_out outarg containing filled-out statx information returned by the server on LOOKUP, MKDIR, MKNOD, etc requests.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v1 10/17] fuse: add struct fuse_entry2_out and helpers for extended entry replies
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds a new extended entry reply struct, fuse_entry2_out, which carries a backing_id and statx attributes. This is necessary for setting up passthrough on inodes. The patch also includes helpers to process fuse_entry2_out for lookup, revalidate, and create operations. Additionally, the fuse_statx_to_attr function is moved earlier in the file to avoid forward declaring.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v1 11/17] fuse: add passthrough lookup
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds support for passthrough lookups in the FUSE file system. When a backing ID is set, it associates the fuse inode with the backing inode to be used for passthrough operations. If no backing ID is set, it caches statx attributes from the reply. The patch modifies several functions in fs/fuse/dir.c to use the new/extended struct fuse_entry2_out for lookups and handle both cases.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v1 12/17] fuse: add passthrough support for entry creation
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds support for passthrough operations in the FUSE file system. When creating a new entry (e.g., file or directory), it uses the extended fuse_entry2_out reply to associate the inode with a backing file if a server returns a backing ID. If no backing ID is returned, statx attributes are cached instead. This patch modifies the create_new_entry function in fs/fuse/dir.c to use the new fuse_entry2_out reply and handle passthrough operations accordingly.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v1 13/17] fuse: add passthrough support for atomic file creation
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds support for atomic file creation through the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) interface. When FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO is enabled, it allows the server to return a backing id, which associates the newly created inode with the backing file for passthrough operations before the dentry is instantiated. The patch modifies the fuse_create_open function to use either the original entry_out structure or the new entry2_out structure depending on whether FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO is enabled. It also introduces a new variable, backing_id, to store the returned backing id from the server. This allows for more efficient and atomic file creation operations.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v1 14/17] fuse: use passthrough getattr in setattr suid/sgid handling
series_items.patch_summary: This patch modifies the FUSE kernel module to use passthrough getattr operations when refreshing inode mode for suid/sgid changes during setattr calls, if the underlying inode supports it.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v1 15/17] fuse: add passthrough setattr
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds the ability to set file attributes directly on a backing inode via notify_change() instead of issuing a FUSE_SETATTR request to the server. This is achieved by introducing passthrough setattr functionality, which updates the backing inode's attributes and then synchronizes the fuse inode's vfs-visible attributes with a passthrough getattr call. The patch modifies several files in the fs/fuse directory, including dir.c, fuse_i.h, and passthrough.c, to implement this new feature.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v1 16/17] fuse: add passthrough open
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds a new feature to the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) kernel module called passthrough open. When enabled, it allows the kernel to skip sending certain operations like FUSE_OPEN and FUSE_OPENDIR to the server and instead opens the backing file directly. This is done by setting the FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_OP_OPEN flag in the ops mask of the backing map. The patch also includes changes to handle passthrough open for directories, regular files, and atomic truncate operations. It aims to improve performance by reducing the number of operations sent to the server.

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch adds documentation to the Linux kernel's FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) module, specifically about extended passthrough mode (FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO). This mode allows for inode operations and directory operations to be passed through from the server to the client, enabling a one-to-one mapping between fuse inode numbers and backing file inode numbers. The documentation provides instructions on how to enable this feature in both the kernel and the FUSE server.

patches_acked.subject: Re: [PATCH] fuse: drop redundant check in fuse_sync_bucket_alloc()
